title: ranunculus minutus var. exilis
intro: Ranunculus minutus var. exilis, also known as small-flowered ranunculus, is a variety of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus minutus var. exilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, infused in oil for topical applications, or made into a salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
